understanding.function: Nuclear fuel reprocessing system for extracting fissile material (uranium and plutonium) from spent nuclear fuel waste, capable of processing multiple fuel types (UOX, MOX, ERU/REU) with 1800 tHM/year capacity
understanding.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing and fissile material recovery
constraints: Product purity standards for downstream fuel fabrication
uncertainties: Specific technical processes and equipment details not defined
Exact safety and regulatory requirements beyond ALARA principle
Interface specifications with upstream waste reception and downstream fuel fabrication systems
Detailed operational parameters and control systems
Emergency response and degraded operation procedures

candidates.name: PUREX Process
candidates.description: Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction process - the most widely used industrial method for reprocessing spent nuclear fuel to separate uranium and plutonium using solvent extraction with tributyl phosphate
candidates.function_alignment: Direct match - designed for uranium/plutonium extraction from spent fuel with industrial scale capability
candidates.notes: Established technology with complexant-based stripping improvements for U/Pu separation

candidates.name: Spent Fuel Dissolution and Reprocessing Systems
candidates.description: Comprehensive reprocessing systems that handle dissolution of spent nuclear fuel and subsequent separation processes for fissile material recovery
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel processing
candidates.function_alignment: High alignment - covers complete reprocessing workflow from dissolution to separation
candidates.notes: Addresses multiple fuel types and industrial scale processing requirements

candidates.name: ALARA-Based Radiation Protection Systems
candidates.description: Radiation protection systems designed around As Low As Reasonably Achievable principles for nuclear reprocessing facilities, including occupational dose reduction measures
candidates.function_alignment: Essential supporting system - directly addresses ALARA constraint for reprocessing facility operations
candidates.notes: Specific focus on spent nuclear fuel reprocessing facility protection aspects

candidates.name: Radioactive Organic Solvent Treatment Systems
candidates.description: Advanced treatment systems for managing spent radioactive organic solvents generated during nuclear fuel reprocessing operations
candidates.function_alignment: Supporting system - handles waste streams from reprocessing operations
candidates.notes: Recent 2024 advances in treatment of reprocessing plant waste solvents

candidates.name: Hot Cell Remote Handling Systems
candidates.description: Remote handling and manipulation systems for nuclear fuel reprocessing operations in highly radioactive environments, enabling safe material processing
candidates.function_alignment: Essential supporting technology - enables safe handling of spent fuel and fissile materials
candidates.notes: Critical for maintaining ALARA principles and operational safety in high-radiation environments

candidates.name: Multi-Fuel Type Processing Systems
candidates.description: Flexible reprocessing systems capable of handling different fuel types including UOX (Uranium Oxide), MOX (Mixed Oxide), and ERU/REU (Enriched Reprocessed Uranium) fuels
candidates.function_alignment: High alignment - directly addresses requirement for processing UOX, MOX, and ERU/REU fuel types
candidates.notes: Addresses the specific constraint for multi-fuel type processing capability

candidates.name: Industrial Scale Solvent Extraction Plants
candidates.description: Large-scale solvent extraction facilities designed for high-throughput nuclear fuel reprocessing with capacities in the range of 1000+ tHM/year
candidates.function_alignment: High alignment - designed for industrial scale throughput matching 1800 tHM/year requirement
candidates.notes: Proven technology for large-scale fissile material recovery operations

candidates.name: Nuclear Facility Containment Systems
candidates.description: Engineered containment systems for nuclear reprocessing facilities providing radiological isolation and emergency response capabilities
candidates.function_alignment: Essential supporting system - provides radiological containment during operations as required
candidates.notes: Critical for meeting radiological safety and regulatory compliance requirements

candidates.name: PUREX (Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Aqueous reprocessing method using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene to separate uranium and plutonium from fission products through solvent extraction. Industry standard for commercial reprocessing with proven track record at facilities like La Hague and Sellafield.
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - chemical separation processes
candidates.function_alignment: Direct match - primary commercial technology for uranium/plutonium extraction from spent fuel with multi-fuel type capability
candidates.notes: Mature technology with established safety protocols, regulatory acceptance, and capacity scalability to 1800 tHM/year

candidates.name: COEX (Co-Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Modified PUREX process that co-extracts uranium and plutonium together rather than separately, reducing proliferation risks while maintaining reprocessing efficiency. Developed by AREVA/Orano for enhanced security.
candidates.function_alignment: High alignment - addresses fissile material recovery with enhanced security features suitable for commercial scale operations
candidates.notes: Reduces separated plutonium inventory, maintains compatibility with existing PUREX infrastructure

candidates.name: Advanced PUREX with Centrifugal Contactors
candidates.description: Enhanced PUREX process using centrifugal contactors instead of pulsed columns for solvent extraction, providing better mass transfer, reduced solvent degradation, and improved process control for high-throughput operations.
candidates.function_alignment: High alignment - improves processing efficiency and capacity while maintaining proven PUREX chemistry
candidates.notes: Better suited for high-capacity operations like 1800 tHM/year, improved operational availability

candidates.name: GANEX (Group Actinide Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Advanced reprocessing concept that co-extracts all actinides (U, Np, Pu, Am, Cm) together using diamide extractants, enabling partitioning and transmutation strategies while recovering fissile materials.
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - advanced partitioning processes
candidates.function_alignment: Moderate alignment - recovers fissile materials but focuses on minor actinides, may exceed basic U/Pu recovery requirements
candidates.notes: More complex than required but offers future flexibility for advanced fuel cycles

candidates.name: Pyroprocessing (Electrochemical Reprocessing)
candidates.description: High-temperature electrochemical process using molten salt electrolytes to separate actinides from fission products. Operates at 500°C using LiCl-KCl eutectic salt with inherent proliferation resistance.
candidates.function_alignment: Moderate alignment - recovers fissile materials but typically produces mixed actinide products rather than separated U/Pu
candidates.notes: Compact footprint, inherent proliferation resistance, but limited commercial deployment experience

candidates.name: UREX+ Process Suite
candidates.description: Modular aqueous reprocessing approach starting with uranium extraction (UREX), followed by optional additional separation steps. Designed for flexible implementation based on fuel cycle strategy and proliferation concerns.
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - modular separation processes
candidates.function_alignment: High alignment - specifically designed for uranium recovery with optional plutonium separation, adaptable to different fuel types
candidates.notes: Modular design allows tailoring to specific requirements, developed with proliferation resistance considerations

candidates.name: Remote Handling and Hot Cell Systems
candidates.description: Integrated remote handling infrastructure including master-slave manipulators, overhead cranes, and shielded hot cells for safe processing of highly radioactive spent fuel materials while maintaining ALARA principles.
candidates.function_alignment: Essential supporting technology - required for safe operation of any reprocessing system handling spent fuel
candidates.notes: Critical for ALARA compliance and operational safety, must integrate with chosen chemical process

candidates.name: Continuous Monitoring and Safeguards Systems
candidates.description: Integrated nuclear material accountancy and control systems including real-time monitoring, material balance areas, and IAEA safeguards compliance for tracking fissile materials throughout the reprocessing facility.
candidates.domain: Nuclear security - safeguards and material control
candidates.function_alignment: Essential supporting system - required for regulatory compliance and material accountability in fissile material processing
candidates.notes: Mandatory for commercial reprocessing operations, must be integrated from design phase

ranked.name: PUREX (Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction) Process
ranked.description: Aqueous reprocessing method using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene to separate uranium and plutonium from fission products through solvent extraction. Industry standard for commercial reprocessing with proven track record at facilities like La Hague and Sellafield.
ranked.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - chemical separation processes
ranked.gap_analysis: Direct match for uranium/plutonium extraction with proven industrial scale capability and multi-fuel type processing. Fully addresses core function requirements.
ranked.notes: Mature, commercially proven technology with established safety protocols and regulatory acceptance. Scalable to required capacity.

ranked.name: Advanced PUREX with Centrifugal Contactors
ranked.description: Enhanced PUREX process using centrifugal contactors instead of pulsed columns for solvent extraction, providing better mass transfer, reduced solvent degradation, and improved process control for high-throughput operations.
ranked.gap_analysis: Improves upon proven PUREX chemistry with enhanced equipment for better capacity and operational availability. Well-suited for 1800 tHM/year requirement.
ranked.notes: Evolutionary improvement of established technology with better performance characteristics for high-capacity operations.

ranked.name: Industrial Scale Solvent Extraction Plants
ranked.description: Large-scale solvent extraction facilities designed for high-throughput nuclear fuel reprocessing with capacities in the range of 1000+ tHM/year
ranked.gap_analysis: Directly addresses industrial scale throughput requirement of 1800 tHM/year. Proven technology for large-scale fissile material recovery operations.
ranked.notes: Established industrial approach with demonstrated capacity scaling and operational experience.

ranked.name: COEX (Co-Extraction) Process
ranked.description: Modified PUREX process that co-extracts uranium and plutonium together rather than separately, reducing proliferation risks while maintaining reprocessing efficiency. Developed by AREVA/Orano for enhanced security.
ranked.gap_analysis: Addresses fissile material recovery with enhanced security features. Compatible with existing infrastructure while reducing separated plutonium inventory.
ranked.notes: Evolutionary development of PUREX with enhanced security characteristics, suitable for commercial scale operations.

ranked.name: Multi-Fuel Type Processing Systems
ranked.description: Flexible reprocessing systems capable of handling different fuel types including UOX (Uranium Oxide), MOX (Mixed Oxide), and ERU/REU (Enriched Reprocessed Uranium) fuels
ranked.gap_analysis: Directly addresses the specific constraint for processing UOX, MOX, and ERU/REU fuel types. Essential for meeting multi-fuel capability requirement.
ranked.notes: Addresses critical system requirement for handling diverse fuel types with established technology base.

ranked.name: Spent Fuel Dissolution and Reprocessing Systems
ranked.description: Comprehensive reprocessing systems that handle dissolution of spent nuclear fuel and subsequent separation processes for fissile material recovery
ranked.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel processing
ranked.gap_analysis: Covers complete reprocessing workflow from dissolution to separation, addressing multiple fuel types and industrial scale processing requirements.
ranked.notes: Comprehensive system approach covering full reprocessing chain with industrial scale capability.

ranked.name: Hot Cell Remote Handling Systems
ranked.description: Remote handling and manipulation systems for nuclear fuel reprocessing operations in highly radioactive environments, enabling safe material processing
ranked.gap_analysis: Essential supporting technology enabling safe handling of spent fuel and fissile materials. Critical for maintaining ALARA principles.
ranked.notes: Essential infrastructure component for safe operations in high-radiation environments, well-established technology.

ranked.name: Nuclear Facility Containment Systems
ranked.description: Engineered containment systems for nuclear reprocessing facilities providing radiological isolation and emergency response capabilities
ranked.gap_analysis: Provides required radiological containment during operations. Essential supporting system for meeting safety and regulatory compliance requirements.
ranked.notes: Critical safety infrastructure with established design principles and regulatory framework.

ranked.name: ALARA-Based Radiation Protection Systems
ranked.description: Radiation protection systems designed around As Low As Reasonably Achievable principles for nuclear reprocessing facilities, including occupational dose reduction measures
ranked.gap_analysis: Directly addresses ALARA constraint for reprocessing facility operations. Essential supporting system for regulatory compliance.
ranked.notes: Mandatory safety system with established implementation approaches and regulatory requirements.

ranked.name: Radioactive Organic Solvent Treatment Systems
ranked.description: Advanced treatment systems for managing spent radioactive organic solvents generated during nuclear fuel reprocessing operations
ranked.gap_analysis: Supporting system for handling waste streams from reprocessing operations. Important for overall facility operation but not core to fissile material extraction function.
ranked.notes: Important auxiliary system for waste management with recent technological advances, supports overall facility operation.
